conditions.name: heterogeneous_baseline
conditions.rationale: Realistic mixed fleet (each truck draws its own hub + trailer) on the canonical world. The headline test: does global coordination beat greedy when trucks mostly serve different equipment markets?

conditions.name: homogeneous_contention
conditions.rationale: Every truck pinned to hot-shot (HS) on a deliberately THIN board (25 loads/snapshot). The whole fleet contends for one scarce equipment market - a genuine demand>supply squeeze where conflict-free greedy degrades and global coordination should matter most.

conditions.name: high_density
conditions.rationale: Flooded board (120 loads/snapshot). With options everywhere, greedy rarely collides - the coordination edge may compress.

conditions.name: competition
conditions.rationale: 40% of visible loads are skimmed first by rival demand (view-biased toward contested loads). A thinner shared board - does coordination still help when the best loads vanish for everyone?

conditions.name: expensive_miles
conditions.rationale: Fuel 0.55 -> 0.95 $/mi and empty miles burn 1.5x (rebuilds the cost stack). Every wasted deadhead hurts more, so better global assignment of trucks to nearby loads should pay off.
